fuzzer_role.value_profile_cmplog: primary pair A — vpc; gains gradient from CMP_MAP on the table-lookup CMP
fuzzer_role.cmplog: primary pair B — cmp; I2S has no literal to substitute (DFA transitions are table lookups), reduces to naive baseline

expected_direction: vpc > cmp; vpc/cmp ratio grows monotonically with KEYWORD_LEN as cmp's geometric 1/256^KEYWORD_LEN per-attempt rate falls below the 600s budget while vpc's per-character CMP_MAP gradient compounds linearly

expected_curve_per_fuzzer.value_profile_cmplog: graceful degradation across KEYWORD_LEN; non-zero at every scan value (gradient retains intermediate-state seeds character-by-character)
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.cmplog: geometric decay matching naive (~600s/256^KEYWORD_LEN expected hits); resolves trivially at KEYWORD_LEN=1, drops sharply at KEYWORD_LEN>=3

acceptance.rule: vpc median > 0 at KEYWORD_LEN=3 AND cmp median = 0 at KEYWORD_LEN=3 AND vpc/cmp ratio monotone non-decreasing across KEYWORD_LEN

design_basis: Hand-rolled DFA with KEYWORD_LEN+1 states; transitions encoded in a runtime-initialised table to prevent the compiler from folding per-character literal CMPs. The gating CMP is `dfa_table[state*256+c] != REJECT_SENTINEL` — both operands runtime-derived array values. cmplog's I2S logs `next` and `REJECT` (=0xFF) as cmp1 operands; substituting 0xFF into input bytes does not advance the DFA, so I2S provides zero useful entries for the keyword. value_profile's CMP_MAP records Hamming distance between the table-lookup result and the reject sentinel; intermediate-state seeds (state=1 after 'd' lands at offset 0) are retained, giving vpc a per-character gradient. Mirrors flex-generated lex scanners (libpcap scanner.c case-arms) where the DFA traversal CMP `yy_chk[base+c] != state` has the same shape. Also mirrors BPF-optimizer bitmask accumulator at libpcap optimize.c:650 (br1077) where the runtime-built `use` mask is structurally similar (table-lookup-against-mask).
notes: Primary comparison: vpc vs cmp on the value_profile axis. They differ by ONE component (CMP_MAP added to cmplog) — same axis as i2s_corpus_pollution but a different I2S-failure regime.

Distinct from i2s_corpus_pollution (case (c) — dictionary pollution by N noise CMPs) and from vp_gradient_derived_operand (case (b) on the (vp, naive) pair with bijective derivation to a literal target). This template covers case (b) on the (vpc, cmp) pair where the gating CMP is a TABLE LOOKUP and there is no literal target at all — pure I2S blackout.
KEYWORD_LEN is COMPILE-TIME parameter; one Dockerfile per scan value at /home/miao/libafl_fuzzbench/docker/targets/Dockerfile.vp_rescues_i2s_derived_operand_kw{1,2,3,5}.

Design history: br1077 agent originally proposed a bijective XOR chain (DERIVATION_DEPTH knob). Replaced 2026-05-04 with br736's DFA design (KEYWORD_LEN knob) — more faithful to both libpcap origins (BPF optimizer bitmask AND flex DFA scanner). Both br1077 and br736 are listed as real-target origins.
